name: Rehabilitation Sciences Resume Sample

experience.company: Binghamton General Hospital, United Health Services
experience.title: Occupational Therapy Intern
bullets: Conducted assessments for approximately 20 patients weekly to evaluate physical, cognitive, and psychosocial abilities, using standardized tools and observation to inform treatment plans
Implemented personalized therapeutic interventions for activities of daily living such as dressing, bathing, and meal preparation to improve patient independence
Educated patients and families on the use of adaptive equipment, such as grab bars and reachers, enhancing safety and functionality within their homes
Collaborated with a multidisciplinary team, including physical therapists, speech-language pathologists, and case managers, to develop and execute comprehensive patient care plans

experience.title: Occupational Therapy Intern
bullets: Supported school-based occupational therapy services for students ages 5-12 by assisting with assessments to evaluate students' fine motor, visual-motor, and sensory processing skills
Implemented targeted interventions under supervision, including handwriting exercises, sensory regulation activities, and classroom-based skill-building tasks
Modified classroom materials and workstations to support students' functional independence and active participation in academic tasks
Collaborated directly with teachers and occupational therapists to reinforce strategies and ensure consistent application of therapy goals within the classroom

experience.title: Research Assistant
bullets: Contributed to a faculty-led research study examining how simulation-based learning enhances clinical reasoning skills in occupational therapy students, assisting with study design and implementation
Conducted literature reviews and synthesized findings to support the development of simulation scenarios and assessment tools grounded in current evidence
Collected and analyzed quantitative and qualitative data on student performance and feedback, identifying key themes to inform recommendations for curriculum improvement

experience.title: Conference Presenter, 20XX Education Summit
bullets: Developed presentation materials for session titled "Integrating Simulation-Based Learning to Build Clinical Reasoning in Occupational Therapy Students," synthesizing current research findings
Designed interactive elements for the session, including small-group reflection prompts and debriefing questions to demonstrate how simulation can be applied across different stages of OT education
Co-presented the session at the AOTA Education Summit alongside a faculty mentor, sharing outcomes from a pilot project and discussing best practices for integrating simulation into OT curricula

experience.title: Shift Lead
bullets: Built strong relationships with a team of 12 baristas and 200+ daily customers by actively listening and responding to needs, fostering collaboration and creating a positive work environment
Addressed 15-20 customer and operational issues per week by identifying problems quickly and implementing solutions, ensuring smooth store operations and maintaining customer satisfaction
Trained and coached 12 team members using tailored communication and guidance strategies to support different learning styles
metrics_mentioned: 15-20 customer and operational issues per week

experience.title: Barista
bullets: Provided personalized service to 150+ daily customers by actively listening to their preferences and handling special requests, ensuring a positive customer experience
Maintained a clean, organized, and well-stocked workspace by sanitizing equipment, restocking supplies, and monitoring inventory, supporting smooth operations and minimizing service delays
Managed 20-30 simultaneous drink and food orders during peak hours by prioritizing tasks, coordinating with team members, and streamlining workflow

experience.title: Nursing Assistant, Psychiatric Unit
bullets: Provided compassionate care to patients on a psychiatric unit and collaborated with the interdisciplinary healthcare team to adapt individualized patient care plans
Conducted patient rounds, documented all findings in patient charts, and communicated effectively with nurses, psychiatrists, other healthcare professionals, patients, and families as appropriate
Employed de-escalation techniques and crisis intervention strategies to address and manage challenging behaviors, maintaining a focus on patient safety

experience.title: Mentor
bullets: Fostered a supportive and personalized mentorship experience for an eighth-grade student at Johnson City Middle School, aiding in a smooth transition to high school by providing individualized guidance and emotional support
Guided mentee in establishing clear academic and personal objectives on a weekly basis, cultivating a sense of accountability and drive for achievement in their high school endeavors

Stony Brook University, State University of New York
